## 3. Priorities & Struggles

**What Julien said matters most (his own words, highest trust):**
> "It's not about the games I develop today. It's not about any of my dozens of projects. It's about what I'm going to start doing seriously in 6 months. Right now, it's only about understanding what's happening, understanding what's worth pursuing and what is not. And I have no idea. That's why I'm trying so many things. I need to be at the edge."

This is the master frame. Everything else is subordinate to it. He is in an **orientation phase**, not an execution phase. The proliferation of projects is intentional reconnaissance, not distraction.

**What's hard:**
- Fundamental uncertainty about the first real offer, for whom, at what price.
- No clear signal yet on which direction earns the next 6 months of serious effort.
- The re-compass (Mar 30) shows disciplined pruning — he knows what *not* to do — but the positive bet is still fuzzy.

**What he's avoiding:**
- Premature commitment to any single project track before conviction arrives.
- Heroic operator behavior at the shop.
- Over-engineering the system instead of doing the work (Mar 30 re-compass may have been partly productive procrastination — or necessary re-grounding; unclear).
